opp.ais_why:
Avoma returned zero meetings for this opportunity; no transcript evidence of AI discussion.

Email evidence is procurement logistics only; the buyer is described as 'very strict in terms of following procurement policy, and they basically... will not deviate.'

Tender scope centres on COTS SaaS, OCDS/public-notice publishing and accessibility—no AI capability requirement evidenced. Low confidence.

sources: SF Description (tender scope: COTS SaaS, OCDS, accessibility)
metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists in any source: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c and Value_to_Customer__c are unread or empty, no Avoma calls were discovered, and no task or Next Step carries a cost, headcount, cycle-time or savings figure. The qualitative driver is clear from the opportunity name (replacing the incumbent Pecos/Proactis platform) and the tender scope (COTS SaaS, OCDS/public-notice publishing, accessibility), but the financial impact of the current system's gaps and the ROI Zycus would deliver are unknown. In a government procurement the business case is central to scoring, so the immediate move is to secure the published evaluation criteria and weightings and anchor value to them.

champion.narrative: No champion exists: no contact role is marked Champion, no field names one, and with zero Avoma calls there is no evidence of advocacy (introductions, sharing internal criteria, defending Zycus against rivals). The most plausible cultivation targets from the roster are Laura Martin (Team Lead, Evaluator) and Steven Patterson (Procurement Development Manager, Evaluator), who are close enough to the evaluation to have visibility without being arms-length, but neither has shown any advocacy signal. In a strict, consensus-driven tender this is the single most damaging gap because it leaves Zycus blind to scoring and shortlist status. The move is a value-led approach (a comparable UK public-sector reference or case study) to earn one insider who will share evaluation status.

sources: Opportunity name (Proactis is the incumbent Pecos provider)
competition.narrative: Zycus is one bidder in a competitive formal tender against JAGGAER (a strong UK/EU public-sector procurement platform and the primary named rival), Proactis (the incumbent Pecos provider being displaced, per the opportunity name and Competitors__c) and at least one undisclosed competitor (Unknown/Won't Share). All three come from Competitors__c; no call, task or Next Step adds fresh competitive intelligence, so relative positioning is unvalidated. The incumbent cuts both ways: the buyer has issued a replacement tender (dissatisfaction), but incumbent inertia and switching cost remain a do-nothing risk if the ITT stalls. The wedge is public-sector platform breadth and OCDS/accessibility fit rather than AI, and it must be evidenced against the published ITT criteria, not assumed.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is a mandate to replace the incumbent Pecos/Proactis Purchase-to-Contract platform, evidenced by the opportunity name and the live replacement tender, with public-sector-specific needs around OCDS/public-notice publishing and accessibility conformance (SF Description). What is inferred rather than stated is the operational reason for the replacement (capability gaps, cost, or end-of-life), because no discovery call or buyer quote articulates it. The pain owner is the eProcurement/eCommerce function (Barry Dalziel, Deputy Director of eProcurement; Nicola Fobister, eCommerce Development Manager). The move is a compliant discovery touch to capture, in the buyer's words, what is driving the replacement and how success will be measured.

sources: SF Description (COTS SaaS replacement of Pecos)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown: there is no visibility into the framework/call-off vehicle, MSA/SOW path, legal or InfoSec review, or the ERP/systems-integration scope for replacing the Pecos platform, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c was not recovered. Because this is a public-sector tender, the paper process will be governed by the ITT terms and standard Scottish Government contract conditions rather than a negotiated MSA, which raises the importance of reading the ITT documentation carefully. The move is to obtain the ITT contract schedule and standstill/award timetable so the paper path and any InfoSec/accessibility conformance requirements are known before shortlist.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (Debra MacLeod, Head of Finance Projects & MI, primary)
Field history: Amount 750k to 2M on 9 Feb 2026
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic authority is structurally mapped but not engaged: Nick Ford (Director, Procurement & Property) sits as Executive Sponsor on the contact roles, so the budget-owning role is identified, but no field, task or call shows him engaging Zycus on value or validating the 2M budget (raised from 750k on 9 Feb 2026). Debra MacLeod (Head of Finance Projects & MI, primary contact) is the finance evaluator most recently in the thread (the 20 Mar RFI emails). No pricing or commercial conversation is documented with either. The move is to convert Nick Ford's sponsorship into an active value/budget conversation and confirm who holds award authority within the tender governance.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a strict formal government tender: the buyer is described as very strict on procurement policy and will not deviate, running an RFI followed by a Source-to-Contract ITT that was to open 24 Mar 2026 (20 Mar email from Debra MacLeod via PCST). The buying group is large and consensus-driven: two Decision Makers (Nicola Fobister, James Hall), an Executive Sponsor (Nick Ford), two senior Influencers (Nikki Archer, Scott Bell) and several eProcurement/finance Evaluators. What is unknown is the ITT timeline beyond opening, the shortlist gate, and award authority, because Zycus has no champion inside the evaluation. The move is to secure the tender timeline and gates in writing through the compliant procurement channel.

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are partly visible from the tender structure rather than from buyer dialogue: the scope centres on COTS SaaS, OCDS/public-notice publishing and accessibility (SF Description), which are the explicit public-sector fit requirements Zycus must evidence. What is not confirmed is the formal ITT scoring model, weightings and any pass/fail gates, because there are no discovery calls and no champion feeding back internal deliberations. The named AI/Merlin capability in the product set is not a stated tender requirement, so it should not be assumed to score. The move is to obtain the published ITT evaluation criteria and map the response to each weighted line.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — Three competitors are on record and none has fresh 2026 buyer evidence, so no rival is buyer-preferred on the current read. JAGGAER is the strongest current threat as an entrenched UK/EU public-sector procurement platform in a live tender; Proactis is the incumbent being displaced (dissatisfaction signalled by the replacement ITT, but incumbent inertia is a real do-nothing risk); the undisclosed third bidder is a blind spot. With zero call intelligence and no champion, the ranking rests on structural evidence, not buyer statements, and must be validated once a compliant contact line is restored.
bullets.text: A competitor is pushing back — Three competitors are on record and none has fresh 2026 buyer evidence, so no rival is buyer-preferred on the current read.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: The Government of Scotland — Formal Evaluation · $2M · closes 31 Dec

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 21 is carried by exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum is flat at 50: no forward or backward motion was observed.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 4 is anchored on the stage win (21), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has been silent for 105 days since the 20 Mar RFI submission; the only subsequent changes are internal rep-side and forecast edits. On a strict government tender some silence is procedural, but with no champion the cooling cannot be distinguished from disqualification.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 11 Aug 2022 (deal age about 1,422 days), in Formal Evaluation. Last verified buyer activity 20 Mar 2026, 105 days before the 3 Jul 2026 sweep, well past the 60-day cold threshold that forces Off Track. Close date 31 Dec 2026 is 181 days out and has already slipped four times (20 Dec 2025 to 31 Mar 2026 to 30 Sep 2026 to 31 Dec 2026 on 12 May 2026). Amount was raised from 750k to 2M on 9 Feb 2026. The 20 Mar activity was the buyer confirming the Source-to-Contract ITT would open 24 Mar 2026; nothing two-way has been logged in the 105 days since. Government tenders run mandated no-contact windows, so some silence is procedural, but with no champion and no shortlist confirmation Zycus cannot tell whether it is progressing or already out. Required pace: an ITT of this size normally moves to shortlist within 90-120 days of submission; at 105 days with zero signal the deal is at or past that window with no confirmation.
evidence: Cold forcing condition: 105 days since the last verified buyer activity (LastActivityDate 20 Mar 2026), past the 60-day threshold. This alone forces Off Track at an early stage.

No champion identified or cultivated across contact roles, MEDDPICC fields, tasks or calls. In a formal government evaluation with 12 named buyer contacts, no internal advocate means zero visibility into scoring or shortlist status.
No economic-buyer, budget or pricing dialogue documented anywhere. The 2M amount (raised from 750k on 9 Feb 2026) is unvalidated by the buyer.
Competing against JAGGAER (strong UK public-sector platform), Proactis (the incumbent being displaced) and at least one undisclosed rival, with no call intelligence on relative positioning.
Zero Avoma calls discovered and AIS Negative (3.0): the tender is COTS SaaS, OCDS publishing and accessibility, not AI, so the Merlin narrative is not the buying driver here.

Forecast indefensible: a blind, cold deal with no champion, no EB engagement and no shortlist confirmation cannot sit in Pipeline with confidence; recommend Omit pending re-engagement and a confirmed ITT/shortlist outcome.
north_star_verdict.headline: A live $2M government tender, but Zycus is flying blind: 105 days of buyer silence since the 20 Mar RFI submission, no champion, no economic-buyer dialogue, and no visibility into shortlist or scoring against JAGGAER and the incumbent Proactis.

flags: No champion inside a 12-person committee: cultivate Laura Martin or Steven Patterson with a UK public-sector reference to regain visibility into scoring.
Blind for 105 days post-submission: re-open a compliant contact line to confirm the ITT shortlist status and next-stage timeline.
Merlin/AI is not the buying driver (AIS Negative, no AI in tender): lead value on OCDS/accessibility and platform breadth versus JAGGAER and the incumbent Pecos gaps.
best_practice_check.summary: The three highest-leverage plays to raise win probability now: earn an insider for visibility, restore a compliant contact line, and position on public-sector fit rather than AI.

competitive_position.summary: Three competitors are on record and none has fresh 2026 buyer evidence, so no rival is buyer-preferred on the current read. JAGGAER is the strongest current threat as an entrenched UK/EU public-sector procurement platform in a live tender; Proactis is the incumbent being displaced (dissatisfaction signalled by the replacement ITT, but incumbent inertia is a real do-nothing risk); the undisclosed third bidder is a blind spot. With zero call intelligence and no champion, the ranking rests on structural evidence, not buyer statements, and must be validated once a compliant contact line is restored.
